content: Population-level cancer prevention interventions: (1) Tobacco control—comprehensive MPOWER measures reducing lung and multiple other cancers; (2) Vaccination programs—HPV vaccination (9-valent vaccine preventing 90% HPV-related cancers), HBV vaccination (universal infant vaccination); (3) Screening programs—organized population-based screening with quality assurance: mammography (biennial 50-74 years), cervical cytology/HPV testing (21-65 years), colonoscopy/FIT (45-75 years), low-dose CT for high-risk smokers; (4) Policy interventions—alcohol taxation and availability restrictions, trans fat bans, processed meat labeling, occupational exposure limits, air quality standards; (5) Sun safety—shade structures, sunscreen access, tanning bed restrictions for minors; (6) Chemoprevention—aspirin for colorectal cancer in select populations, tamoxifen/raloxifene for high-risk breast cancer. Navigation programs improve screening completion. Overdiagnosis/overtreatment risks require balanced communication.
